zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Fitzwalter於1430年出生時，她的父親Baron William Fitzwalter Baron30歲，母親Elizabeth Chidiock26歲。她於1444年10月27日與John Radcliffe Esq.在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1513年在英國英格蘭艾可武德哈姆 沃爾特享年83歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fitzwalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Fitzwalter was born in 1430, her father, Baron William Fitzwalter Baron, was 30 and her mother, Elizabeth Chidiock, was 26. She married John Radcliffe Esq. on 27 October 1444, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1513, in Woodham Walter, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 83.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Fitzwalterが1430年に生まれた時，父Baron William Fitzwalter Baronは30歳，母Elizabeth Chidiockは26歳であった。1444年10月27日にイギリスイギリスエセックスでJohn Radcliffe Esq.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1513年にイギリスイギリスエセックスウッドハム ワルターで83歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Fitzwalter(이)가 1430년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baron William Fitzwalter Baron(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Chidiock(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1444년 10월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 John Radcliffe Esq.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1513년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 우드함 월터에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Fitzwalter родилась в 1430 г., её отцу по имени Baron William Fitzwalter Baron было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Chidiock, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Radcliffe Esq. 27 октября 1444 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1513 г. в местности вудхам валтир, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет.
